nodes.label: Concentric Circles Patterning Path & Liftoff Signals Plot
nodes.norm_label: concentric circles patterning path & liftoff signals plot

nodes.label: Concentric Circles Patterning
nodes.rationale: Generating concentric circles requires discrete paths centered at a common coordinate. This design patterns concentric circles of radii 500, 1000, and 1500 microns centered at (10000, 10000) microns.

nodes.label: Drawing Path (flag=0)
nodes.rationale: Flag=0 denotes active drawing/patterning periods where the lithography system exposes the material along the circular path.

nodes.label: Travel/Liftoff Path (flag=1)
nodes.rationale: Flag=1 is used to transition between different concentric circles without exposure (shutter closed / liftoff). The travel path is plotted as a horizontal red dashed line connecting the boundaries of consecutive circles at Y=10000.

nodes.label: Pattern Vertices
nodes.rationale: Vertices are individual discrete coordinate points representing the path trajectory. The system interpolates or moves point-to-point through these coordinates during the patterning process.

nodes.label: Exposure Segmentation Mechanism
nodes.rationale: Segmenting exposure via flags (drawing vs travel) allows a continuous sequence of coordinates to describe complex geometries while preventing stray exposure during positional transitions.

nodes.label: Successful Circular Trajectory
nodes.rationale: Microscope image showing complete circle of radius 500 microns drawn after the firmware speed override fix.

nodes.label: Exposure & Line Thickness Variation
nodes.rationale: Observation that the line quality is thicker in top-left and thinner/fading in the bottom-right, suggesting substrate tilt or Z-stage contact force variance.

nodes.label: Stepping Smoothness at 0.2 µm Resolution
nodes.rationale: Close-up view of the drawn line confirming the 0.2 micron step resolution of the stage does not introduce visible staircase artifacts.

nodes.label: Concentric Ghost Circle Trace
nodes.rationale: Close-up showing a faint concentric dotted track inside the main path, which likely is a result of prior failed attempts or calibration residuals.

nodes.label: Motor_Init.vi
nodes.rationale: No global objects; init in startup state only to fix Python's module-level side effects.

nodes.label: Motor_CheckLimits.vi
nodes.rationale: Reject user inputs moving motor more than 50,000 steps and implement serial port reset to prevent controller unresponsiveness on limit switches.

nodes.label: SMU_Close.vi
nodes.rationale: Always include 100ms delay between serial close/open to prevent port lockups.

nodes.label: ManualMove.vi
nodes.rationale: Always read back actual steps after move to prevent missing position updates in contact_point_move.
